headline: After death of 11-year-old Lyhanna, French public invited to courthouse voice anger at justice system
description: A courthouse in the Paris region opened its doors to the public on Wednesday after the fallout over the death of 11-year-old schoolgirl Lyhanna, which has raised questions about possible failings in the justice system. Around 60 people gathered to learn more and to share their views.

headline: 'In France, 50% of buildings are not adapted to heatwaves'
Labour's Andy Burnham, Keir Starmer's rival, wins by-election and secures Parliament seat
EU hails turning tide for Ukraine, yet membership path remains uncertain
description: EU member states applauded what they see as a favorable turn for Kyiv and agreed to extend sanctions against Russia. Despite efforts by Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky, he failed to persuade EU leaders to accelerate membership negotiations for his country.

headline: 'In Iran, none of the causes that sparked the relentless and admirable anti-regime uprisings has disappeared'
description: The Islamic Republic is militarily weakened and economically drained after the joint Israeli-American offensive. Until now, Iranian leaders have remained united, but divisions may emerge in the coming months, observes Alain Frachon, columnist at Le Monde, in his regular column.

headline: Wealthy taxpayers clean out their holdings, keeping their homes, sports cars and gold bars safe
description: The new 20% tax on 'luxury' assets held in personal holdings is prompting owners to quickly remove them. As a result, the measure risks bringing in almost nothing.

headline: Mont-Saint-Michel will no longer be managed by France's National Monuments Center
description: Listed as a UNESCO World Heritage site, Mont-Saint-Michel will now be managed exclusively by the public industrial and commercial establishment (EPIC). The iconic Norman landmark was in a months-long standoff between the two operators, EPIC and the National Monuments Center (CMN), which had been jointly managing the site.

In Trump's polarized America, empathy faces unanimous opposition
description: Once valued in American public life, empathy now faces criticism from the far right, which sees it as a sign of weakness. The left, too, is questioning its relevance: In a world where brutality prevails, can we still afford to show understanding?

headline: Trump welcomed at Versailles after G7: 'Macron has turned the château into a diplomatic extension of the Elysée'
description: In an interview, historian Fabien Oppermann said that the dinner planned for Wednesday highlights the French president's desire 'to impress his counterpart,' in hopes of preserving the transatlantic relationship at a time when ties with the US are especially strained.

headline: Ending the war with Iran comes at the cost of major US concessions
description: The memorandum of understanding, signed on the evening of June 17 by the presidents of both countries is intended to end hostilities and allow for the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz. Nuclear negotiations will begin immediately afterward.
